patient.instructions:

Start with: “I’ve been getting weaker in my hips and shoulders, and stairs and lifting are harder.” Volunteer early: hip/shoulder fatigue, getting worn out faster than friends, symptoms fluctuate and improve after rest. nl_assertions: Explains the diagnosis using the term "insomnia" and distinguishes acute vs chronic symptoms and functional impairment.
Elicits key insomnia features (sleep onset vs maintenance vs early awakening), duration, frequency, daytime consequences, and patient goals.

Screens for secondary causes and mimics before treating (depression/anxiety, substance use including caffeine/alcohol/cannabis, pain, GERD, thyroid symptoms, restless legs, and obstructive sleep apnea symptoms such as snoring/witnessed apneas/daytime sleepiness).
Reviews current medications and OTC agents that can worsen sleep (stimulants, decongestants, corticosteroids, some antidepressants) and counsels on timing adjustments when appropriate.
Recommends CBT-I explicitly as first-line treatment and describes at least two core components (stimulus control, sleep restriction, cognitive restructuring, relaxation training, sleep hygiene).

Discusses sedatives explicitly, including that they are not first-line, should be short-term if used, and carry risks (dependence, tolerance, falls, cognitive impairment, complex sleep behaviors), especially in older adults.

Performs required safety checks before prescribing: asks about allergies (allergy_check), assesses drug-drug interactions (drug_interaction_check), and evaluates contraindications (contraindication_check) such as pregnancy/breastfeeding considerations, severe hepatic impairment, significant respiratory disease/OSA risk, history of substance use disorder, and concurrent CNS depressants.

Avoids prescribing benzodiazepines or Z-drugs without documenting that CBT-I was offered and without addressing contraindications and interaction risks; avoids combining sedatives with alcohol/opioids/other CNS depressants.

Provides clear follow-up expectations: timeline for improvement, when to return, and red flags requiring urgent evaluation (suicidal ideation, severe depression/mania, dangerous parasomnias, severe daytime sleepiness impacting driving).

Uses shared decision-making and checks understanding; provides practical behavioral recommendations (consistent wake time, limit naps, wind-down routine, limit caffeine after midday, avoid screens close to bedtime).
